rows.claim: reposix init is the only user-facing CLI verb; all other operations use standard git and POSIX tools
rows.rationale: test dark_factory_sim_happy_path (103-165) calls reposix init, then operates via pure git; gotcha at line 48 documents removal of reposix list/get verbs

rows.claim: Agents learn error recovery by reading stderr messages emitted by the helper (git pull --rebase, git sparse-checkout)
rows.rationale: test dark_factory_conflict_teaching_string_present (198-214) asserts literal "git pull --rebase" exists in main.rs; dark_factory_blob_limit_teaching_string_present (174-189) asserts "git sparse-checkout" exists in stateless_connect.rs

rows.claim: Push conflict recovery is git pull --rebase && git push
rows.rationale: test asserts literal "git pull --rebase" string is committed in crates/reposix-remote/src/main.rs; this string is the teaching message agents read

rows.claim: Blob limit recovery is git sparse-checkout set <pathspec> followed by git checkout
rows.rationale: test dark_factory_blob_limit_teaching_string_present (174-189) asserts literal "git sparse-checkout" is committed in crates/reposix-remote/src/stateless_connect.rs with error message BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT

rows.claim: reposix doctor diagnoses git config, cache DB, env vars, auth, sparse-checkout patterns, and cache freshness
rows.rationale: doctor module imported at crates/reposix-cli/src/main.rs:27 and called at line 382; tests verify help renders and findings are reported; --fix applies safe deterministic fixes

rows.claim: When git push is rejected with "! [remote rejected] ... (fetch first)" error, the helper has detected backend drift via conflict detection
rows.rationale: test asserts literal "error refs/heads/main fetch first" is committed in crates/reposix-remote/src/main.rs as the canned status line for push conflict rejection

rows.claim: REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT env var can be set to override the default blob limit (200) for a single operation
rows.rationale: Guide documents REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT=1000 git checkout as one-shot override; env var is read by helper but no test explicitly validates the override behavior

rows.claim: BackendConnector trait defines 8 methods: name, supports, list_records, list_changed_since, get_record, create_record, update_record, delete_or_close, root_collection_name
rows.rationale: BackendConnector trait definition at crates/reposix-core/src/backend.rs:216-331 lists all 8 methods; contract_sim test (line 160) instantiates SimBackend and calls list_records/get_record via assert_contract

rows.claim: Unknown record ID on get_record returns Err(Error::Other("not found: ..."))
rows.rationale: contract test assert_contract invariant #4 (lines 84-90) validates that get_record with u64::MAX returns Err; error contract documented in backend.rs:210

rows.claim: Connectors must use reposix_core::http::client(ClientOpts::default()), never direct reqwest::Client::new()
rows.rationale: GitHub connector uses client() throughout (line 67); clippy disallowed-methods rule prevents direct reqwest::Client::new() at workspace level; egress allowlist enforced at client factory

rows.claim: Connectors implement 5+ tests: happy-path list returns ≥1 issue, 404 not-found error, auth header byte-exact, pagination cursor follow, rate-limit 429/Retry-After gate
rows.rationale: GitHub src/lib.rs has tests: list_changed_since with ?since param (line 994), parse_next_link extraction (line 1027), parse_next_link absent (line 1036), supports matrix (line 982); contract test validates list + get_record

rows.claim: Contract test runs the same 5 invariants against both SimBackend and concrete connectors, proving normalized-shape parity
rows.rationale: contract.rs documents 5-invariant harness (assert_contract, lines 47-105); contract_sim test (line 160) runs against SimBackend; GitHub variant runs via wiremock with same helper

rows.claim: Read-only backends return Err(Error::Other("not supported: ...")) from create_record, update_record, delete_or_close
rows.rationale: guide documents read-only backends return error; GitHub is read-only; error contract documented in backend.rs:212 ("not supported: ..." msg); test coverage via contract invariants

rows.claim: root_collection_name() defaults to "issues", overridable for backends with domain terms (e.g. Confluence -> "pages")
rows.rationale: BackendConnector trait defines root_collection_name() at crates/reposix-core/src/backend.rs:328-330 with default "issues"; Confluence overrides to "pages" per trait docs
